title: Overview
summary: Agent-native sandbox infrastructure: interfaces, lifecycle, templates, browser handoff, identity, and plan limits—plus how Integrate guides and Core Workflows map to CLI, REST API, and Python SDK.

title: Sandbox Lifecycle
summary: Create, list, inspect, stop, start, and delete sandboxes. Sandboxes only consume Compute Units while running.

title: Browser Handoff
summary: Let humans enter the sandbox in a browser: authenticated session or owner-shared JWT link; full workspace view and human-in-the-loop control.

title: API Keys & Auth
summary: Sign up via CLI or Console OAuth (Google/GitHub), then session login, API keys, control-plane vs data-plane access, and selected sandbox grants.

title: Usage & Limits
summary: Read plan limits, compute remaining, storage quota, allowed templates, concurrency limits, and billing-period status.

title: CLI Guide
summary: Install the CLI and core behavior: global flags, credential precedence, JSON vs human output, --help, skills, defaults, and local config.

title: REST API Guide
summary: REST shape: base URL, /v1 and /health, headers, JSON, errors, OpenAPI, control vs data plane — not duplicate workflows.

title: Python SDK Guide
summary: Install treadstone-sdk, AuthenticatedClient, module layout, sync vs detailed vs asyncio, errors, regeneration — not duplicate workflows.

title: API Reference
summary: The user-facing control-plane contract: auth, sandboxes, browser handoff, proxy, usage endpoints, pagination, and error shape.

title: CLI Reference
summary: Root options, command groups, auth precedence, JSON mode, config keys, and the exact hosted CLI surface.

title: Python SDK Reference
summary: Real import paths, generated endpoint modules, request and response models, and sync or async call shapes.

title: Error Reference
summary: The stable JSON error envelope, common public error codes, and the recovery step that matches each failure.
